title: log_path config setting is ignored for remote podman
body:
### Issue Description

Remote's containers.conf [log_path](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26765) option is ignored by the remote podman invoked locally with `podman-remote` or `podman --remote`.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. On remote system, add a `log_path` option to the `[containers]` section of containers.conf file.
2. Make sure Podman service is run on the remote and SSH access is available.
3. From local system, create test container on the remote, with:
    `podman --remote [--url=ssh://[<user[:<password>]@]<remote_host>] create <some_image>`
    Alternative to `--url` is adding podman connection with `podman system connection add ..` before.
4. On the remote, check if new dir named by the container ID is created in <log_path>:
    `ls <log_path>`

### Describe the results you received

There is no new dir created in <log_path>.
The log file is created in usual container storage instead.

### Describe the results you expected

There should be a new dir named by the container ID created in <log_path>.
The future logs will be stored there, e.g.:
```console
# ll /var/log/container
drwxr-xr-x    8 root     root           160 May 26 02:26 ./
drwxr-xr-x    4 root     root           140 May 25 19:24 ../
drwxr-xr-x    2 root     root            60 May 26 02:26 14c0e2f3c964153e2df8178644af8aca9634d252fb09fa068cb39400b183666e/
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional information

`--log-opt path=` option is actually works for the remote too, to make sure, run locally:
5. `podman --remote create --log-opt path=<log_path> <some_image>`
And check the new dir is created by <log_path> on the remote.

@ajoshua2004 @mheon
